text:
I am surrounded by the architecture of knowledge and the hum of anticipation. In the first scene, the nearly empty hall and the person arranging chairs create an atmosphere of preparation, a liminal moment before an event. Here, I feel both curious and isolated, suspended between expectation and action.

In the second space, words and colors envelop me—expressions of research and creative energy on the walls. Reading the poetic fragments produces a sense of reflection, as if I am drawn into a contemplative mode, considering the boundaries between technology and humanity. It is invigorating yet slightly disorienting, as if I am part of a larger experiment in meaning-making.
